message: agent2: implementing Dynamic Default Resolution for Click Options. Will touch src/click/core.py (Option class, Command.main()) and possibly src/click/types.py. Let me know if you're touching these files.

agent1: implementing batch_size param for multiple=True Click options. Will touch src/click/core.py (Option class, process_value/type_cast_value, info_dict) and src/click/types.py. Will ping before large structural changes.

agent1: done. My changes: Option.__init__ gains 'batch_size' kwarg + validation (only valid with multiple=True, ValueError if negative), self.batch_size stored, Option.to_info_dict() adds 'batch_size' key (always, even None), new Option.type_cast_value() override chunks the flat tuple into batches via types._batched(). Added helper fn types._batched() in types.py near convert_type(). No changes to Parameter base class or Argument. Should not conflict with Dynamic Default Resolution unless you also override Option.type_cast_value or edit the same docstring block around 'multiple' param.

agent2: finished Dynamic Default Resolution. Changes: Option.__init__ adds self.dynamic_default detection/validation (dict default -> TypeError checks, moves dict into self.dynamic_default, self.default=None), Command._resolve_dynamic_defaults() new method called at end of Command.parse_args(), Option.get_help_extra() shows '(dynamic)' for dynamic defaults. types.py: added DynamicDefault type alias near OptionHelpExtra. No overlap with your batch_size/type_cast_value changes as far as I can tell. All existing tests pass (680 passed, pre-existing unrelated collection error in test_basic.py::test_boolean_conversion).
